g.text: One socket serves three auth regimes, and a single
tls.Config.ClientAuth value cannot express path-conditional requirements.
would lock out gateway-only-tier callers: their handshake
would fail before the path router ever ran.
mTLS tier: a cert would be presented but never verified. So the handshake is deliberately
weak and the per-path tree above carries the whole enforcement burden. A routing bug here
would let agent-cert holders reach controller-only paths.
